title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: vignette walks create_*() functions build PK-Sim Building blocks named arguments, add_*() / remove_*() mutators attach snapshot. use Midazolam template throughout:

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_individual() builds Individual demographic arguments. PK-Sim seeds underlying physiological tree simulation time. Extra demographics (e.g. gestational_age) accepted additional named arguments: Attach result snapshot add_individual(): Parameters sit specific path inside individual’s physiological tree built create_parameter() using path argument. PK-Sim writes path pipe-separated segments (Organism|Liver|...): can pass list parameters straight create_individual(parameters = ...), assign individual$parameters fact (see “Modifying existing building blocks” ).
code: patient <- create_individual(   name = "Patient_001",   species = "Human",   population = "European_ICRP_2002",   gender = "FEMALE",   age = 35,   weight = 65,   height = 165 )  patient #>  #> ── Individual: Patient_001 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Species: Human #> • Population: European_ICRP_2002 #> • Gender: FEMALE #> • Age: 35 year(s) #> • Height: 165 cm #> • Weight: 65 kg pregnant_patient <- create_individual(   name = "Pregnant_Patient",   species = "Human",   population = "European_ICRP_2002",   gender = "FEMALE",   age = 28,   weight = 70,   height = 168,   gestational_age = 20 # weeks )  pregnant_patient #>  #> ── Individual: Pregnant_Patient ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Species: Human #> • Population: European_ICRP_2002 #> • Gender: FEMALE #> • Age: 28 year(s) #> • Gestational Age: 20 week(s) #> • Height: 168 cm #> • Weight: 70 kg add_individual(snapshot, patient) snapshot$individuals #>  #> ── Individuals (3) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) #> • Korean (Yu 2004 study) #> • Patient_001 ehc <- create_parameter(   path = "Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction",   value = 0.4 )  gfr <- create_parameter(   path = "Organism|Kidney|GFR",   value = 120,   unit = "ml/min",   source = "Literature",   description = "Normal adult GFR",   source_id = 123 )  gfr #>  #> ── Parameter: Organism|Kidney|GFR  #> • Value: 120 #> • Unit: ml/min #> • Source: Literature #> • Description: Normal adult GFR

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_formulation() covers every PK-Sim release profile ("Dissolved", "Weibull", "First Order", "Zero Order", "Lint80", "Particles", "Table"). accepted parameters depend chosen type: "Dissolved" takes none; kinetic profiles ("Weibull", "First Order", "Zero Order", "Lint80", "Particles", "Table") accept release-kinetics parameters specific profile (example "Weibull" formulation accepts dissolution_time, dissolution_shape, lag_time, …). See ?create_formulation parameter list per type.
code: oral_solution <- create_formulation(   name = "Oral Solution 10mg/mL",   type = "Dissolved" )  oral_solution #>  #> ── Formulation: Oral Solution 10mg/mL ────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Dissolved  tablet <- create_formulation(   name = "Immediate Release Tablet",   type = "Weibull",   parameters = list(     dissolution_time = 30,     dissolution_time_unit = "min",     lag_time = 5,     lag_time_unit = "min",     dissolution_shape = 1.2,     suspension = TRUE   ) )  tablet #>  #> ── Formulation: Immediate Release Tablet ─────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Weibull #>  #> ── Parameters ── #>  #> • Dissolution time (50% dissolved): 30 min #> • Lag time: 5 min #> • Dissolution shape: 1.2 #> • Use as suspension: 1 add_formulation(snapshot, oral_solution) add_formulation(snapshot, tablet)

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_compound() builds compound validation common fields molecular_weight_unit. Compound-level parameters (clearances, halogens, …) passed list create_parameter() results: parameters named compound identified name, omit path argument.
code: drug <- create_compound(name = "Drug X")  drug <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   is_small_molecule = TRUE,   molecular_weight = 250.3,   plasma_protein_binding_partner = "Albumin",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Cl_spec", value = 5, unit = "ml/min/kg")   ) )  drug #>  #> ── Compound: Drug X ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Basic Properties ── #>  #> • Type: Small Molecule #> • Plasma Protein Binding Partner: Albumin #> • Molecular Weight: 250.3 g/mol #>  #> ── Physicochemical Properties ── #>  #> ── Additional Parameters ── #>  #> • Additional Parameters (1 total): #>   • Cl_spec: 5 ml/min/kg [Unknown]

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_process() builds compound process (plasma protein binding, metabolism, clearance, …) named arguments. internal_name identifies process template PK-Sim’s compound process repository (example "SpecificBinding", "MetabolizationSpecific_MM", "GlomerularFiltration"); data_source labels data origin. See ?create_process usage current examples; canonical list internal_name values maintained PK-Sim groups categories surfaced process$category (protein_binding_partners, metabolizing_enzymes, hepatic_clearance, transporter_proteins, renal_clearance, biliary_clearance, inhibition, induction). $category active binding derives domain label ("protein_binding_partners", "metabolizing_enzymes", "hepatic_clearance", "transporter_proteins", "renal_clearance", "biliary_clearance", "inhibition", "induction") internal_name. Compound processes loaded snapshot exposed flat named list Process objects via compound$processes, long-form processes tibble returned get_compounds_dfs() surfaces every (process, parameter) pair row.
code: binding <- create_process(   internal_name = "SpecificBinding",   data_source = "Buhr 1997",   molecule = "GABRG2" )  metabolism <- create_process(   internal_name = "MetabolizationSpecific_MM",   data_source = "Optimized",   molecule = "CYP3A4",   metabolite = "Hydroxy-Drug",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Km", value = 1.2, unit = "µmol/l"),     create_parameter(name = "kcat", value = 9.865, unit = "1/min")   ) )  metabolism$category #> [1] "metabolizing_enzymes"

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_population() builds Population recipe: settings used PK-Sim sample cohort simulation time. Use range() age, weight, height, BMI bounds.
code: adults <- create_population(   name = "Healthy Adults",   number_of_individuals = 50,   proportion_of_females = 50,   species = "Human",   source_population = "European_ICRP_2002",   age_range = range(20, 60, "year(s)"),   weight_range = range(50, 90, "kg") )  adults #>  #> ── Population: Healthy Adults ── #>  #> Source Population: European_ICRP_2002 #> Number of individuals: 50 #> Proportion of females: 50% #> Age range: 20 - 60 year(s) #> Weight range: 50 - 90 kg

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_expression_profile() builds ExpressionProfile. identity profile composite Molecule|Species|Category, three required, along molecule type.
code: cyp3a4 <- create_expression_profile(   molecule = "CYP3A4",   species = "Human",   category = "Healthy",   type = "Enzyme",   ontogeny = "CYP3A4" )  cyp3a4 #>  #> ── Expression Profile: CYP3A4 (Enzyme) ───────────────────────────────────────── #> • Species: Human #> • Category: Healthy #> • Ontogeny: CYP3A4

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_protocol() builds Protocol. default creates Simple Protocol (one application type, one dose, optional dosing interval); pass schemas create Advanced Protocol.
code: single_dose <- create_protocol(   name = "Single dose 10mg",   application_type = "Oral",   dosing_interval = "Single",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Start time", value = 0, unit = "h"),     create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 10, unit = "mg")   ) )  single_dose #>  #> ── Protocol: Single dose 10mg ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Simple #> • Application Type: Oral #> • Dosing Interval: Once #>  #> ── Parameters ── #>  #> • Start time: 0 h #> • InputDose: 10 mg

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: dosing schedules fit single application (multi-day course, two compounds dosed staggered schedules), use Advanced Protocol. Advanced Protocol built one Schemas; schema describes repeating block applications (example “10 mg orally every 24 hours 5 days”). Inside schema, individual application SchemaItem. build create_schema() (repeating block) create_schema_item() (one application), pass list schemas create_protocol(): application_type schema item validated canonical PK-Sim application types ("Oral", "IntravenousBolus", "IntravenousInfusion", "Intramuscular", "Subcutaneous", "Dermal", "Rectal", "Inhalation", "Intraperitoneal").
code: qd_oral <- create_protocol(   name = "Once daily oral, 5 days",   schemas = list(     create_schema(       name = "Schema 1",       parameters = list(         create_parameter(name = "NumberOfRepetitions", value = 5),         create_parameter(name = "TimeBetweenRepetitions", value = 24, unit = "h")       ),       items = list(         create_schema_item(           name = "Item 1",           application_type = "Oral",           parameters = list(             create_parameter(name = "Start time", value = 0, unit = "h"),             create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 10, unit = "mg")           )         )       )     )   ),   time_unit = "h" )  qd_oral #>  #> ── Protocol: Once daily oral, 5 days ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Advanced (Schema-based) #> • Time Unit: h #>  #> ── Schemas ── #>  #> • Schema: Schema 1 #>   • Item 1: Oral -

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: Event discrete perturbation drug administration: meal, change posture, enzyme inducer dose, exercise bout. create_event() builds one template (name event recipe PK-Sim’s event database) plus optional parameter overrides. PK-Sim clones named template simulation time applies overrides. set available templates defined inside PK-Sim (particular PK-Sim Events database ships installation), exact list valid template names depends PK-Sim setup. Templates commonly seen published snapshots include “Meal: Standard (Human)” family food effects; need template installation, define PK-Sim first re-export.
code: breakfast <- create_event(   name = "Breakfast",   template = "Meal: Standard (Human)",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Meal energy content", value = 500, unit = "kcal"),     create_parameter(name = "Meal volume", value = 0.3, unit = "l")   ) )  breakfast #>  #> ── Event: Breakfast ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Template: Meal: Standard (Human) #>  #> ── Parameters: ── #>  #> • Meal energy content: 500 kcal #> • Meal volume: 0.3 l

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: Observer simulation-time formula exposes derived quantity (example tissue concentration) natural model output. Observers travel groups called Observer sets. create_observer_set() builds set name list observers. Build observer Observer$new() (validates input gives typed accessors $name, $type, $dimension, $formula, $container_tags), bundle set attach set snapshot:
code: brain_obs <- Observer$new(list(   Name = "brain_plasma_conc",   Type = "Container",   Dimension = "Concentration (molar)",   Formula = list(Formula = "Conc_Br") ))  brain_set <- create_observer_set(   name = "BrainPlasmaConcentration",   observers = list(brain_obs) )  brain_set #>  #> ── ObserverSet: BrainPlasmaConcentration ─────────────────────────────────────── #> • 1 observer  add_observer_set(snapshot, brain_set)

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_observed_data() builds ospsuite::DataSet named arguments time grid, measurement values, units, optional error series. value_dimension required gates unit validation.
code: obs <- create_observed_data(   name = "Phase I - Subject 001",   time = c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 24),   values = c(0, 12.5, 18.2, 15.8, 11.2, 6.8, 3.4, 1.1),   time_unit = "h",   value_unit = "mg/l",   value_dimension = "Concentration (mass)" )  obs #> <DataSet> #>   • Name: Phase I - Subject 001 #>   • X dimension: Time #>   • X unit: h #>   • Y dimension: Concentration (mass) #>   • Y unit: mg/l #>   • Error type: NULL #>   • Error unit: NULL #>   • Molecular weight: NULL #>   • LLOQ: NULL #> Meta data:

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: building-block kind paired add_*() remove_*() mutators take snapshot first return invisibly, chain base pipe. can pass several names remove :
code: remove_individual(snapshot, "Patient_001") remove_formulation(snapshot, "Oral Solution 10mg/mL") remove_individual(snapshot, c("Patient_001", "Patient_002")) remove_expression_profile(snapshot, "CYP3A4|Human|Healthy")

what: Adding several building blocks at once
title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: add_*() accepts either single building block list , can attach many one call. add_*() reports many entries added.
code: patients <- list(   create_individual("Patient_A", age = 25, gender = "MALE"),   create_individual("Patient_B", age = 45, gender = "FEMALE"),   create_individual("Patient_C", age = 65, gender = "MALE") )  add_individual(snapshot, patients)  snapshot$individuals #>  #> ── Individuals (5) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) #> • Korean (Yu 2004 study) #> • Patient_A #> • Patient_B #> • Patient_C

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: fields existing building block mutable; assign like R6 active binding. Reading object shows effect: pattern works nested objects. bump parameter individual, look path individual$parameters write value: every change, parent snapshot exports updated values: export_snapshot(snapshot, ...) writes straight back JSON, extra step required.
code: patient <- snapshot$individuals$Patient_A patient #>  #> ── Individual: Patient_A ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Gender: MALE #> • Age: 25 year(s) patient$age <- 30 patient$weight <- 75 patient$gender <- "FEMALE" patient #>  #> ── Individual: Patient_A ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Gender: FEMALE #> • Age: 30 year(s) #> • Weight: 75 kg korean <- snapshot$individuals$`Korean (Yu 2004 study)` ehc <- korean$parameters$`Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction` ehc #>  #> ── Parameter: Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction  #> • Value: 1 #> • Source: Unknown ehc$value <- 0.4 ehc #>  #> ── Parameter: Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction  #> • Value: 0.4 #> • Source: Unknown

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_*() functions validate common inputs canonical ospsuite enums abort informative messages bad input: checks also exported standalone helpers (validate_species(), validate_gender(), validate_unit(), validate_population()) need outside create_*() call.
code: tryCatch(   {     create_individual(       name = "Invalid Patient",       species = "InvalidSpecies",       gender = "OTHER"     )   },   error = function(e) cat("Error:", e$message, "\n") ) #> Error: Invalid species: InvalidSpecies  tryCatch(   {     create_formulation(       name = "Invalid Form",       type = "NonexistentType"     )   },   error = function(e) cat("Error:", e$message, "\n") ) #> Error: subscript out of bounds

text: vignette("exporting-snapshots--dataframes") covers tibble layer analysis. full API reference https://esqlabs.github.io/osp.snapshots/reference/.

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: osp.snapshots can flatten nested PK-Sim snapshot tree tibbles ready downstream analysis dplyr. vignette covers entry point, per-kind shapes, common analysis patterns.

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles() takes snapshot plus kind naming collection convert, returns either tibble named list tibbles collections split related tables. Valid values kind: "compounds" "events" "expression_profiles" "formulations" "individuals" "observed_data" "observer_sets" "populations" "protocols" tibble layer read-: mutating returned tibble feed changes back snapshot.

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "compounds") returns list two tibbles: $properties carries one row per (compound, parameter) pair across Compound physicochemical properties. $processes long-form view: one row per (compound, process, parameter) triple across every biological Process (metabolism, transport, clearance, binding, …). $processes tibble carries category column mirrors process$category R6 side. Filtering category gives view per-category groups compound:
code: head(compound_dfs$properties) #> # A tibble: 6 × 8 #>   compound  category              type  parameter value unit  data_source source #>   <chr>     <chr>                 <chr> <chr>     <chr> <chr> <chr>       <chr>  #> 1 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… lipo… Optimized 2.89… Log … NA          Param… #> 2 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… frac… Gertz et… 0.031 NA    NA          Param… #> 3 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… mole… NA        325.… g/mol NA          NA     #> 4 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… halo… Cl        1     NA    NA          NA     #> 5 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… halo… F         1     NA    NA          NA     #> 6 Midazolam physicochemical_prop… pKa   base      6.2   NA    NA          NA head(compound_dfs$processes) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   compound  category       process_name parameter value unit  data_source source #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>        <chr>     <chr> <chr> <chr>       <chr>  #> 1 Midazolam protein_bindi… SpecificBin… koff      1     1/min Buhr 1997   Param… #> 2 Midazolam protein_bindi… SpecificBin… Kd        1.8   nmol… Buhr 1997   NA     #> 3 Midazolam renal_clearan… GlomerularF… GFR frac… 0.64… NA    Optimized   Param… #> 4 Midazolam metabolizing_… Metabolizat… In vitro… 850   pmol… Optimized   NA     #> 5 Midazolam metabolizing_… Metabolizat… Km        4     µmol… Optimized   aggre… #> 6 Midazolam metabolizing_… Metabolizat… kcat      8.76… 1/min Optimized   Param… #> # ℹ 3 more variables: molecule <chr>, metabolite <chr>, species <chr> compound_dfs$processes |>   filter(category == "metabolizing_enzymes")

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "individuals") returns three tibbles joinable individual_id: $individuals one row per Individual demographic fields (species, population, gender, age, weight, height, gestational age, disease state, calculation methods). $individuals_parameters long-form view individual’s parameter tree: one row per (individual, parameter path) pair, value, unit, source, value-origin metadata. $individuals_expressions lists expression profiles attached individual: one row per (individual, profile) pair.
code: individual_dfs <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "individuals") names(individual_dfs) #> [1] "individuals"             "individuals_parameters"  #> [3] "individuals_expressions"  head(individual_dfs$individuals) #> # A tibble: 2 × 17 #>   individual_id            name    seed species population gender   age age_unit #>   <chr>                    <chr>  <int> <chr>   <chr>      <chr>  <dbl> <chr>    #> 1 European (P-gp modified… Euro… 1.72e7 Human   European_… MALE    30   year(s)  #> 2 Korean (Yu 2004 study)   Kore… 5.30e7 Human   Asian_Tan… MALE    23.3 year(s)  #> # ℹ 9 more variables: gestational_age <dbl>, gestational_age_unit <chr>, #> #   weight <dbl>, weight_unit <chr>, height <dbl>, height_unit <chr>, #> #   disease_state <chr>, calculation_methods <glue>, #> #   disease_state_parameters <chr> head(individual_dfs$individuals_parameters) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   individual_id                   path  value unit  source description source_id #>   <chr>                           <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>  <chr>           <int> #> 1 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A… Orga…     1 NA    Unkno… NA                 NA #> 2 Korean (Yu 2004 study)          Orga…     1 NA    Unkno… NA                 NA head(individual_dfs$individuals_expressions) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   individual_id                         profile                                  #>   <chr>                                 <chr>                                    #> 1 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) CYP3A4|Human|European (P-gp modified, C… #> 2 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) AADAC|Human|European (P-gp modified, CY… #> 3 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) P-gp|Human|European (P-gp modified, CYP… #> 4 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) OATP1B1|Human|European (P-gp modified, … #> 5 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) ATP1A2|Human|European (P-gp modified, C… #> 6 European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) UGT1A4|Human|European (P-gp modified, C…

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "formulations") returns two tibbles joinable formulation_id: $formulations one row per Formulation name release type. $formulations_parameters long-form view type-specific release parameters: one row per (formulation, parameter) pair, value unit.
code: formulation_dfs <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "formulations") names(formulation_dfs) #> [1] "formulations"            "formulations_parameters" head(formulation_dfs$formulations) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   formulation_id    name              formulation               formulation_type #>   <chr>             <chr>             <chr>                     <chr>            #> 1 Oral solution     Oral solution     Formulation_Dissolved     Dissolved        #> 2 Tablet (Dormicum) Tablet (Dormicum) Formulation_Tablet_Weibu… Weibull head(formulation_dfs$formulations_parameters) #> # A tibble: 4 × 11 #>   formulation_id    name   value unit  is_table_point x_value y_value table_name #>   <chr>             <chr>  <dbl> <chr> <lgl>            <dbl>   <dbl> <chr>      #> 1 Tablet (Dormicum) Diss… 0.0107 min   FALSE               NA      NA NA         #> 2 Tablet (Dormicum) Lag … 0      min   FALSE               NA      NA NA         #> 3 Tablet (Dormicum) Diss… 4.38   NA    FALSE               NA      NA NA         #> 4 Tablet (Dormicum) Use … 1      NA    FALSE               NA      NA NA         #> # ℹ 3 more variables: source <chr>, description <chr>, source_id <int>

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "populations") returns two tibbles joinable population_id: $populations one row per Population sampling-recipe header: source population, individual name, sample size, proportion females, seed, age, weight, height, BMI, eGFR bound columns. $populations_parameters long-form view sampling distributions PK-Sim draw : one row per (population, parameter) pair, distribution_type, statistic, value, unit.
code: population_dfs <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "populations") names(population_dfs) #> [1] "populations"            "populations_parameters" head(population_dfs$populations) #> # A tibble: 0 × 22 #> # ℹ 22 variables: population_id <chr>, name <chr>, seed <int>, #> #   number_of_individuals <int>, proportion_of_females <dbl>, #> #   source_population <chr>, individual_name <chr>, age_min <dbl>, #> #   age_max <dbl>, age_unit <chr>, weight_min <dbl>, weight_max <dbl>, #> #   weight_unit <chr>, height_min <dbl>, height_max <dbl>, height_unit <chr>, #> #   bmi_min <dbl>, bmi_max <dbl>, bmi_unit <chr>, egfr_min <dbl>, #> #   egfr_max <dbl>, egfr_unit <chr>

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "protocols") returns single 13-column tibble whether snapshot protocols . Advanced Protocols contribute one row per Schema item, protocol-level, schema-level, item-level parameters joined name.
code: protocols_df <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "protocols") head(protocols_df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 13 #>   protocol_name   schema_name schema_item_name type  formulation dosing_interval #>   <chr>           <chr>       <chr>            <chr> <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 iv 0.075 mg/kg… NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 2 iv 0.05 mg/kg … NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 3 iv 1 mg (5 min) NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 4 iv 0.001 mg (5… NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 5 iv 1 mg (bolus) NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> 6 iv 2 mg (2 min) NA          NA               Intr… NA          Once            #> # ℹ 7 more variables: start_time <dbl>, start_time_unit <chr>, dose <dbl>, #> #   dose_unit <chr>, rep_number <dbl>, rep_time <chr>, rep_time_unit <chr>

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "observer_sets") returns list two tibbles joinable observer_set_id (observer_set_name): $observer_sets one row per ObserverSet name count observers contains. $observers one row per Observer name, type, dimension, formula, container_tags (|-joined Tag values underlying ContainerCriteria).

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "observed_data") flattens every observed-data series long-form tibble ready plotting.
code: obs_data_df <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "observed_data") head(obs_data_df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 30 #>   name            xValues yValues yErrorValues xDimension xUnit yDimension yUnit #>   <chr>             <dbl>   <dbl>        <dbl> <chr>      <chr> <chr>      <chr> #> 1 Ahonen 1995 - …     0.5 0.0156        0.0231 Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 2 Ahonen 1995 - …     1   0.0280        0.0160 Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 3 Ahonen 1995 - …     1.5 0.0235        0.0108 Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 4 Ahonen 1995 - …     2   0.0185      NaN      Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 5 Ahonen 1995 - …     3   0.0118      NaN      Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> 6 Ahonen 1995 - …     4   0.00848     NaN      Time       h     Concentra… mg/l  #> # ℹ 22 more variables: yErrorType <chr>, yErrorUnit <chr>, molWeight <dbl>, #> #   lloq <dbl>, `DB Version` <chr>, ID <chr>, `Study Id` <chr>, #> #   Reference <chr>, Source <chr>, Grouping <chr>, `Data type` <chr>, N <chr>, #> #   Molecule <chr>, Species <chr>, Organ <chr>, Compartment <chr>, Route <chr>, #> #   Dose <chr>, `Times of Administration [h]` <chr>, Formulation <chr>, #> #   `Food state` <chr>, Comment <chr>

title: Getting started with osp.snapshots

title: Convert a snapshot collection to a tibble or list of tibbles — as_tibbles
text: tibble named list tibbles, depending kind: "compounds": list properties processes tibbles. properties carries one row per (compound, physicochemical property) pair (plus folded process rows backwards compatibility); processes long-form, one row per (compound, process, parameter) triple. "protocols", "observed_data": single tibble. "observer_sets": list observer_sets (one row per ObserverSet) observers (one row per Observer, joinable parent via observer_set_id / observer_set_name). "individuals": list individuals, individuals_parameters, individuals_expressions. "formulations": list formulations, formulations_parameters. "populations": list populations, populations_parameters. "events": list events, events_parameters. "expression_profiles": list expression_profiles, expression_profiles_parameters.

Type formulation, one : "Dissolved" - Immediate release solution "Weibull" - Weibull tablet formulation "Lint80" - Lint80 tablet formulation "Particle" - Particle formulation "Table" - Custom release profile table "ZeroOrder" - Zero-order release formulation "FirstOrder" - First-order release formulation parameters List. named list parameters formulation. valid parameters depend formulation type. Invalid parameters result error.

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: dissolution_time - Time achieve 50% dissolution (numeric, default: 240) dissolution_time_unit - Unit dissolution time (character, default: "min") lag_time - Lag time dissolution starts (numeric, default: 0) lag_time_unit - Unit lag time (character, default: "min") dissolution_shape - Dissolution shape parameter (numeric, default: 0.92) suspension - Whether use suspension (logical, default: TRUE)

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: dissolution_time - Time achieve 80% dissolution (numeric, default: 240) dissolution_time_unit - Unit dissolution time (character, default: "min") lag_time - Lag time dissolution starts (numeric, default: 0) lag_time_unit - Unit lag time (character, default: "min") suspension - Whether use suspension (logical, default: TRUE)

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: thickness - Thickness unstirred water layer (numeric, default: 30) thickness_unit - Unit thickness (character, default: "\u00b5m") distribution_type - Type distribution, "mono" "poly" (character, default: "mono") radius - Particle radius (mean geometric mean) (numeric, default: 10) radius_unit - Unit radius (character, default: "\u00b5m") Parameters polydisperse distribution (distribution_type = "poly"): particle_size_distribution - "normal" "lognormal" (character, default: "normal") radius_sd - Radius standard deviation, normal distribution (numeric, default: 3) radius_sd_unit - Unit radius SD (character, default: "\u00b5m") radius_cv - Coefficient variation, lognormal distribution (numeric, default: 1.5) radius_min - Minimum particle radius (numeric, default: 1) radius_min_unit - Unit minimum radius (character, default: "\u00b5m") radius_max - Maximum particle radius (numeric, default: 19) radius_max_unit - Unit maximum radius (character, default: "\u00b5m") n_bins - Number bins (integer, default: 3)

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: tableX - Vector time points release profile hours (numeric vector, required) tableY - Vector fraction dose time point (numeric vector, required) suspension - Whether use suspension (logical, default: TRUE)

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: end_time - Time administration end (numeric, default: 60) end_time_unit - Unit end time (character, default: "min")

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: thalf - Half-life drug release (numeric, default: 0.01) thalf_unit - Unit half-life (character, default: "min")

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
code: # Create a dissolved formulation (simplest type) dissolved <- create_formulation(name = "Oral Solution", type = "Dissolved")  # Create a Weibull tablet formulation tablet <- create_formulation(   name = "Standard Tablet",   type = "Weibull",   parameters = list(     dissolution_time = 60,     dissolution_time_unit = "min",     lag_time = 10,     lag_time_unit = "min",     dissolution_shape = 0.92,     suspension = TRUE   ) )  # Create a particle formulation with monodisperse distribution particle <- create_formulation(   name = "Suspension",   type = "Particle",   parameters = list(     thickness = 25,     thickness_unit = "\\u00b5m",     radius = 5,     radius_unit = "\\u00b5m"   ) ) #> No distribution_type provided, using default of mono  # Create a table formulation with custom release profile custom <- create_formulation(   name = "Custom Release",   type = "Table",   parameters = list(     tableX = c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8),     tableY = c(0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 0.9, 1.0),     suspension = TRUE   ) )

title: Create a new observed data set — create_observed_data
text: name Character. Name observed data set (required). time Numeric vector. Time grid x-values (required). values Numeric vector. Measurement y-values, length time (required). time_unit Character. Unit time (example "h", "min", "day(s)"). Validated ospsuite::ospUnits$Time. Defaults "h". value_unit Character. Unit values (example "mg/l"). supplied alongside value_dimension, unit validated dimension via validate_unit(). value_dimension Character. Dimension values (example "Concentration (mass)"). Required (default): pass one names ospsuite::ospDimensions. Previously defaulted silently "Concentration (mass)", wrong non-concentration data. error Numeric vector. Optional error y-values, length values. error_type Character. Auxiliary type error, typically one "ArithmeticStdDev", "GeometricStdDev", "ArithmeticStdErr". Defaults "ArithmeticStdDev" error provided. error_unit Character. Unit error. Defaults value_unit. molecular_weight Numeric. Molecular weight attach data set, g/mol. lloq Numeric. Lower limit quantification. metadata Named list. Extended properties (key/value pairs) stored alongside data set.

osp.snapshots (development version)
text: release widens building-block coverage osp.snapshots consolidates public surface. can now build every PK-Sim building block named arguments single create_*() factory family (compounds, events, expression profiles, observed data, observer sets, populations, processes, protocols, schemas, schema items), attach remove paired add_*() / remove_*() mutators chain base pipe, convert collection tibble one dispatched as_tibbles() entry point. Observer sets supported end end (load, mutate, convert, export). Several previously list-shaped fields existing classes now first-class R6 objects typed accessors.

title: osp.snapshots (development version)
text: load_snapshot() now requires PK-Sim v11.2 snapshots newer (Version >= 79). Re-export older projects PK-Sim v11.2+ loading (#52). get_compounds_dfs() returns list two tibbles, properties processes, instead single combined tibble. Update callers df <- get_compounds_dfs(snap) dfs <- get_compounds_dfs(snap); df <- dfs$properties, switch new long-form dfs$processes (#40). compound$calculation_methods returns CalculationMethods R6 object instead plain list class compound_calculation_methods. Read names compound$calculation_methods$names mutate $add(name), $remove(name); $length reports count (#30). compound$processes returns flat named list Process R6 objects (duplicate names disambiguated _{n}). Filter process$category recover equivalent deprecated per-category accessors (#40). create_parameter() writes identifier data$Name plain parameters (path argument) data$Path path-bearing parameters (path argument). Previously every result carried data$Path. Pass path = ... get path-bearing parameter; pass name = ... get plain one (#52).

title: osp.snapshots (development version)
text: add_*() mutators now accept either single building block list building blocks, mirroring remove_*() accepted character vector names since #66. Success messages sides now uniformly report Added N kind(s) / Removed N kind(s) (#92). can now create every kind building block create_*() function attach matching add_*() mutator: create_compound() builds compound named arguments, validation molecular_weight_unit ospsuite::ospUnits$"Molecular weight" (#27, #48). Attach add_compound(), remove name remove_compound() (#39). create_event() builds event template name optional list parameter overrides (#27). Attach add_event(), remove remove_event() (#39). create_expression_profile() builds expression profile, requiring molecule, species, category, type (#27). create_observed_data() builds ospsuite::DataSet named time, value, unit, optional error-series arguments. value_dimension required gates unit validation (#27, #48). Attach add_observed_data(), remove remove_observed_data() (#39). create_observer_set() builds observer set name list observers (#43). Attach add_observer_set(), remove remove_observer_set() (#38). create_population() builds population, taking Range objects age, weight, height, BMI bounds. number_of_individuals must positive integer; proportion_of_females must length-1 number (#27, #48). Attach add_population() (#39). create_process() builds compound process; internal_name data_source validated (#40). See ?create_process list supported internal_name values. create_protocol() builds Simple Advanced protocol. Pass schemas build Advanced protocol (accepts Schema objects create_schema() raw lists); mixing Simple-fields (application_type, dosing_interval, …) schemas errors (#27, #48, #54). Attach add_protocol(), remove remove_protocol() (#39). create_schema() create_schema_item() build repeatable blocks individual applications used inside Advanced protocol. application_type schema item validated canonical PK-Sim application types (#54). can now convert building-block collection tibble one entry point: as_tibbles(snapshot, kind) returns either bare tibble ("protocols", "observed_data") named list related tibbles (every kind). eight existing get_*_dfs() functions remain available thin wrappers (#36). Observer sets now fully supported. ObserverSet exposes observers named list Observer objects name, type, dimension, formula, container_tags. get_observer_sets_dfs() returns two tibbles (observer_sets set-level rows, observers joinable back via observer_set_id / observer_set_name) (#38, #42, #76). Several previously list-shaped fields now first-class R6 objects: compound$calculation_methods individual$origin_data$calculation_methods return CalculationMethods object can inspect ($names, $length) mutate ($add(), $remove()) (#30). individual$origin_data returns OriginData object holding demographic starting point individual (species, population, gender, age, weight, height, gestational age, calculation methods, optional disease state) (#30). Parameters live parameter tree (Individual, ExpressionProfile, Simulation) now LocalizedParameter objects, identified pipe-separated path. Pre-v11 Applications|... segments migrated Events|... load. create_parameter() routes LocalizedParameter called path argument (#31). compound$processes returns named list Process objects, exposing internal_name, data_source, molecule, metabolite, species, parameters, derived category (#40). protocol$schemas returns named list Schema objects, exposing $items list SchemaItem objects (application type, formulation key, target organ compartment, parameters) (#29).

title: osp.snapshots (development version)
text: Building-block collections now share snapshot_collection S3 class single generic print() method. existing per-kind classes (compound_collection, individual_collection, …) preserved marker classes (#34). export_snapshot() now documents mutations DataSet load (e.g. changing xUnit entry snapshot$observed_data) preserved export. exported ObservedData section replayed original snapshot JSON, filtered entries still present remove_observed_data() (#35).

title: osp.snapshots (development version)
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "protocols") (legacy get_protocols_dfs() wrapper) now returns 13 columns whether snapshot protocols . Previously empty-state path returned 18-column tibble disagreed populated path, breaking bind_rows() across mixed snapshots (#56). remove_expression_profile(), remove_formulation(), remove_individual(), remove_observed_data(), remove_population() now report actual number entries removed instead length input vector, success message reads correctly requested name present snapshot (#66). Observed data removed via remove_observed_data() now dropped exported snapshot. Previously export reused full original ObservedData list whenever lazy cache touched, re-introducing removed entries round-trip. fix applies every building-block section: clearing collection via remove_individual(), remove_formulation(), remove_population(), remove_expression_profile() now writes empty section export instead falling back original entries (#35, #59). Single-element JSON arrays remain arrays export/import, allowing exported snapshots load PK-Sim (#23).

title: osp.snapshots 0.2.0
text: New load_snapshot() function import PKSIM project snapshots JSON files, URLs, predefined templates. New export_snapshot() function save modified snapshots back JSON files import PKSIM. Complete R6 class implementations major PKSIM building blocks: Individual, Compound, Formulation, Population, Protocol, Event, ExpressionProfile, Parameter. New data frame conversion functions building block types: get_individuals_dfs(), get_compounds_dfs(), get_formulations_dfs(), get_populations_dfs(), get_protocols_dfs(), get_events_dfs(), get_expression_profiles_dfs(), get_observed_data_dfs(). New creation functions: create_individual(), create_formulation(), create_parameter() building new building blocks. New management functions: add_individual(), add_formulation(), add_expression_profile(), remove_individual(), remove_formulation(), remove_population(), remove_expression_profile(). Full integration ospsuite::DataSet objects observed data. Comprehensive input validation species, populations, genders, units. Time conversion utilities OSPSuite R lubridate formats. Structured print methods building block classes.
